products.name: Microsoft Defender XDR
products.one_line: Extended Detection & Response platform that correlates signals across endpoints, cloud, identity, and email to detect and respond to threats automatically.
products.when_to_use: Organizations with Microsoft 365 / Azure / hybrid infrastructure seeking unified threat visibility and automated response across all attack surfaces.
products.common_confusion: Often confused with Sentinel (SIEM). Defender XDR focuses on curated, actionable incidents; Sentinel is a log aggregation + custom detection platform.

products.name: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint
products.one_line: Endpoint Detection & Response (EDR) platform providing threat prevention, detection, investigation, and response for Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android.
products.when_to_use: Enterprises needing visibility into and control of endpoint devices, hunting for advanced threats, and managing vulnerabilities across heterogeneous OS environments.
products.common_confusion: Not the same as Windows Defender (built-in antivirus). Defender for Endpoint is enterprise-grade EDR with cloud backend, analytics, and integration into Defender XDR.

products.name: Microsoft Defender for Office 365
products.one_line: Email and collaboration threat protection detecting and blocking malware, phishing, and business email compromise in Exchange Online and Teams.
key_capabilities: Anti-phishing and business email compromise detection
products.when_to_use: Microsoft 365 customers requiring advanced email threat protection beyond Exchange Online's built-in capabilities.
products.common_confusion: Exchange Online includes built-in antispam/antimalware; Defender for Office 365 P1/P2 add sandboxing, behavioral analysis, and phishing simulation.

products.name: Microsoft Defender for Cloud
products.one_line: Cloud-native security posture management (CSPM) and cloud workload protection (CWPP) for Azure, AWS, GCP, and on-prem servers.
key_capabilities: Threat protection for VMs, containers, serverless
products.when_to_use: Hybrid and multi-cloud organizations needing unified cloud security, infrastructure hardening, and compliance tracking.
products.common_confusion: Different from Defender for Endpoint: Cloud focuses on infrastructure misconfigs and cloud-native threats; Endpoint focuses on device-level threats.

products.name: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps
products.one_line: Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) detecting anomalies and preventing data exfiltration in SaaS and cloud applications.
key_capabilities: Real-time user and entity behavior analytics
products.when_to_use: Organizations using SaaS (Office 365, Salesforce, Box, Slack) who need visibility and control over cloud app usage and insider threat detection.
products.common_confusion: Works *on top of* cloud apps (sits between user and SaaS); not a replacement for app-native security settings.

products.name: Microsoft Defender for Identity
products.one_line: On-premises Active Directory threat detection and response identifying credential compromise, lateral movement, and advanced attacks.
products.when_to_use: Organizations with on-prem Active Directory who need to detect advanced attacks and suspicious user behavior in the identity layer.
products.common_confusion: Works *with* Defender for Endpoint (device-focused); Defender for Identity focuses on AD traffic and identity-layer threats.

products.name: Microsoft Sentinel
products.one_line: Cloud-native SIEM and SOAR platform ingesting logs from any source, detecting threats via rules and ML, and automating response through playbooks.
key_capabilities: Log ingestion and aggregation (any source, on-prem or cloud)
Detection rules and ML anomaly detection
products.when_to_use: Organizations with non-Microsoft infrastructure, custom log sources, or high data volumes needing flexible SIEM without on-prem infrastructure.
products.common_confusion: Defender XDR is more 'managed' (Microsoft curates high-fidelity incidents); Sentinel is more 'DIY' (customers build rules). Use Sentinel if you have diverse sources.

products.name: Microsoft Entra ID
products.one_line: Cloud-based identity and access management (IAM) service managing user identities, app access, and authentication across cloud and on-prem resources.
key_capabilities: Single sign-on (SSO) to cloud and on-prem apps
products.when_to_use: Any organization using Microsoft 365 or Azure; the foundation for modern identity and Zero Trust security.
products.common_confusion: Formerly 'Azure AD'; renamed to Entra ID in 2023. Entra = broader product family. Entra ID = core IAM service.

products.name: Microsoft Entra ID Protection
products.one_line: Risk-based conditional access detecting compromised identities and risky sign-ins, automatically triggering MFA or blocking access.
key_capabilities: Impossible travel and anomalous location detection
products.when_to_use: Organizations seeking automated response to identity compromise without adding manual review overhead.
products.common_confusion: Part of Entra ID (requires Premium P2); distinct from Defender for Identity (which monitors on-prem AD).

products.name: Microsoft Entra Permissions Management
products.one_line: Least-privilege access solution discovering, remediating, and monitoring overprivileged identities across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
products.when_to_use: Multi-cloud organizations needing to audit and trim standing permissions in cloud IAM (AWS IAM, Azure RBAC, GCP IAM).
products.common_confusion: Not the same as PIM (which manages *temporary* privilege elevation). Permissions Management remediates *standing* overprivilege.

products.name: Microsoft Entra Verified ID
products.one_line: Decentralized identity solution enabling users to present cryptographically-signed credentials without contacting the issuer on each verification.
products.when_to_use: Organizations exploring decentralized identity and privacy-by-design authentication (early adoption, specialized use cases).
products.common_confusion: Distinct from traditional OAuth/SAML; uses W3C standards for self-sovereign identity.

products.name: Microsoft Purview (Data Governance)
products.one_line: Unified data governance platform discovering, cataloging, classifying, and managing data lineage across Microsoft and multi-cloud environments.
key_capabilities: Data asset catalogs and lineage mapping
products.when_to_use: Data-driven organizations needing to understand data flows, classify sensitive assets, and maintain data dictionaries for compliance and analytics.
products.common_confusion: Part of Purview umbrella; different from DLP and Information Protection (which enforce data protection policies).

products.name: Microsoft Purview (Data Loss Prevention)
products.one_line: Policy-driven prevention of sensitive data exfiltration across email, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, and endpoints.
key_capabilities: Endpoint DLP integration with Defender for Endpoint
products.when_to_use: Organizations handling PII, trade secrets, or regulated data (PHI, PCI, GDPR) who need to prevent accidental or intentional exfiltration.
products.common_confusion: Data Loss *Prevention* (DLP) blocks sharing; Data Lifecycle Management deletes old data; both prevent incidents but differently.

products.name: Microsoft Purview (Information Protection)
products.one_line: Sensitivity labels and encryption protecting data through automatic classification, encryption, and access restrictions that travel with files.
key_capabilities: Integration with Office, Outlook, Teams, SharePoint
products.when_to_use: Organizations needing persistent data protection that survives sharing (label and encryption follow file outside organization).
products.common_confusion: Differs from DLP: Information Protection *encrypts* and *marks* data; DLP *blocks* sharing. Often used together.

products.name: Microsoft Purview (Insider Risk Management)
products.one_line: UEBA-powered detection of risky user behaviors (data exfiltration, unauthorized access, policy violations) with privacy-by-design and case management.
key_capabilities: Behavioral risk templates (departing employees, data leaks, security policy violations)
products.when_to_use: Organizations seeking to detect and prevent insider threats while balancing security with employee privacy.
products.common_confusion: Different from Defender for Cloud Apps (which detects cloud app anomalies). Insider Risk focuses on user behavioral patterns across M365.

products.name: Microsoft Purview (eDiscovery)
products.one_line: Automated data collection, preservation, and discovery for litigation, investigations, and regulatory requests across Microsoft 365 and on-prem sources.
products.when_to_use: Legal, compliance, and investigation teams conducting e-discovery, litigation, or forensic investigations.
products.common_confusion: eDiscovery is a capability within Purview; not a standalone tool. Premium eDiscovery adds ML-powered relevance ranking.

products.name: Microsoft Purview (Compliance Manager)
products.one_line: Streamlined compliance management mapping Microsoft and customer controls to regulatory frameworks, tracking implementation, and providing audit readiness.
key_capabilities: Pre-built assessment templates (GDPR, HIPAA, SOC 2, ISO 27001, etc.)
products.when_to_use: Compliance officers and audit teams needing to manage multi-framework compliance, track control status, and prepare for audits.
products.common_confusion: Not the same as Compliance Insights (cloud app compliance); Compliance Manager is a framework-driven control tracker.

products.name: Microsoft Priva
products.one_line: Privacy automation solution enabling organizations to fulfill subject rights requests (data access, deletion) and conduct privacy assessments under GDPR/CCPA.
products.when_to_use: Organizations operating in EU/California/other jurisdictions with privacy regulations requiring automated fulfillment of data subject rights.
products.common_confusion: Different from Purview DLP/Information Protection; Priva is *reactive* (responds to subject requests) vs. Purview *proactive* (prevents exfiltration).

products.name: Microsoft Intune
products.one_line: Cloud-based mobile device management (MDM) and application management (MAM) for Windows, macOS, iOS, Android, and Linux.
key_capabilities: Mobile app management (MAM) for app-level data protection
products.when_to_use: Organizations managing heterogeneous devices, requiring BYOD support, or needing app-level data protection on personal devices.
products.common_confusion: MDM (device-level controls) vs. MAM (app-level controls). Intune supports both; MAM protects corp data even on unmanaged personal devices.
